mentions.note: CEO Brian Niccol fixing execution and throughput issues; Cramer expects this to be the comeback year.
CEO Brian Niccol turnaround progressing; up to $94 intraday, closed $93.28; slow step-by-step recovery from $70s; restaurant group resurgence underway.
Wildly overbought; reports Wednesday then investor day Thursday (structure confusing). Can't tell good enough story to justify recent ramp unless insanely strong numbers.

Brian Nickel turnaround working: throughput fixed, transactions (rewards + non-rewards) growing for first time in 8 quarters. Menu simplified; morning daypart won back. Investor day tomorrow. Entry point fair; turn durable.

Throughput turnaround under CEO Brian Nickel working; same-store sales beat; first growth in both rewards and non-rewards transactions in eight quarters. Menu simplification, consistent service, bigger rosters drive durability; Cramer holding large position; bullish long-term if you're not yet in.
Tariff headwinds and store optimization challenges; must close underperformers, expand middle America; CEO Brian Nickel taking right approach but execution takes time.
Stock creeping higher; this level challenging historically but ready to break out.

Beat + raised guidance; Brian Niccol turnaround fixing service and hospitality culture first, margins will recover to 13%+ from 10%; loyalty program stagnant until now (pre-order rolling out); stock up 8% today, more upside as analysts upgrade on margin recovery.
